description: Fix validated bugs found during systematic backend code audit: unsafe type casts in browser tools that crash on unexpected extension responses, and stale network capture state that persists across extension disconnects and tab closes.

userStories.title: Validate dispatchToExtension response shape in export-har before accessing .requests and .frames
userStories.description: As a developer using browser_export_har, I want the tool to handle unexpected extension responses gracefully so that I get a clear error message instead of a cryptic 'Cannot read properties of undefined' crash.
acceptanceCriteria: export-har handler validates that requestsResult has a 'requests' array before calling .map() — uses Array.isArray() check with ?? [] fallback, matching the pattern used in analyze-site/index.ts lines 954-956
export-har handler validates that framesResult has a 'frames' array before calling .map() — same defensive pattern
If the response shape is unexpected (e.g., null, error object, missing field), the handler falls back to an empty array instead of crashing
Existing export-har tests continue to pass with valid responses

userStories.title: Clear activeNetworkCaptures on extension disconnect and tab close
userStories.description: As a developer using network capture tools, I want the activeNetworkCaptures set to be cleaned up when the extension disconnects or tabs are closed so that export_har doesn't pass its guard check for stale capture sessions that no longer exist.
acceptanceCriteria: state.activeNetworkCaptures is cleared in the WebSocket close handler (createHandleWsClose in http-routes.ts) when the extension disconnects — all capture sessions are lost when the extension disconnects since they are extension-side state
When browser_close_tab is called, the closed tab's ID is removed from state.activeNetworkCaptures if present
After extension disconnect + reconnect, browser_export_har correctly rejects with 'Network capture is not active' instead of dispatching to the extension and getting a debugger error
userStories.notes:
The activeNetworkCaptures Set<number> on ServerState tracks which tabs have active network capture. It is only modified in two places:
- enable-network-capture.ts:52 (.add)
- disable-network-capture.ts:18 (.delete)

But capture sessions are extension-side state that is lost when:
1. The extension disconnects (WebSocket close) — ALL captures are lost
2. A tab is closed — that tab's capture is lost

Fix in two places:

1. platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts, createHandleWsClose function (~line 584): add `state.activeNetworkCaptures.clear()` alongside the existing pendingDispatches/pendingConfirmations cleanup.

2. platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/close-tab.ts: after the dispatchToExtension call succeeds, add `state.activeNetworkCaptures.delete(args.tabId)` to clean up any capture session for the closed tab. Read close-tab.ts first to see the exact handler structure.
